chunk:
Plant: Tomato (Solanum lycopersicum). Family: Nightshade.
Companions: Basil, Carrots, Parsley, Marigold, Asparagus, Celery, Onions.
Antagonists: Fennel, Broccoli, Cabbage, Corn, Potatoes, Kohlrabi.
Why companions help: Basil repels aphids and may improve flavor; Carrots loosen soil; Marigolds deter nematodes; Parsley attracts beneficial insects
Why avoid antagonists: Fennel secretes growth inhibitors; Brassicas compete for nutrients; Corn attracts same pests; Potatoes share blight disease
Growing: Full sun, water High, spacing 24 inches, Heavy feeder.
Attracts: Bees.

Plant: Basil (Ocimum basilicum). Family: Mint.
Companions: Tomato, Pepper, Oregano, Asparagus, Marigold.
Antagonists: Sage, Rue, Common Thyme.
Why companions help: Improves tomato growth and flavor; Repels flies, mosquitoes, and aphids from neighbors
Why avoid antagonists: Sage and rue inhibit basil growth
Growing: Full sun, water Medium, spacing 12 inches, Light feeder.
Pests deterred: Aphids, Mosquitoes, Flies, Spider mites.
Attracts: Bees, Butterflies.

Plant: Carrots (Daucus carota). Family: Umbellifers.
Companions: Onions, Leeks, Rosemary, Sage, Lettuce, Tomatoes, Chives.
Antagonists: Dill, Parsnips, Celery.
Why companions help: Onions and leeks mask carrot scent from carrot fly; Rosemary and sage deter carrot fly; Lettuce provides shade for roots
Why avoid antagonists: Dill cross-pollinates and may stunt growth; Parsnips compete and share diseases; Celery competes for nutrients
Growing: Full sun, water Medium, spacing 3 inches, Light feeder.
Attracts: Parasitic wasps, Lacewings.

Plant: Beans (Phaseolus vulgaris). Family: Legume.
Companions: Corn, Squash, Carrots, Cabbage, Cucumber, Cauliflower, Radishes.
Antagonists: Onions, Garlic, Peppers, Sunflowers, Chives, Leeks.
Why companions help: Fixes nitrogen in soil for heavy feeders; Corn provides natural trellis for climbing varieties; Three Sisters planting with corn and squash
Why avoid antagonists: Alliums inhibit bean growth; Sunflowers release growth-inhibiting chemicals
Growing: Full sun, water Medium, spacing 6 inches, Nitrogen fixer.
Attracts: Bees.

Plant: Corn (Zea mays). Family: Grass.
Companions: Beans, Squash, Cucumber, Melons, Peas, Pumpkin, Zucchini.
Antagonists: Tomatoes, Celery, Brassicas.
Why companions help: Provides natural trellis for beans and climbing plants; Benefits from nitrogen fixed by beans; Squash shades soil below
Why avoid antagonists: Tomatoes and corn attract corn earworm; Celery and brassicas compete for nutrients
Growing: Full sun, water High, spacing 12 inches, Heavy feeder.

Plant: Squash (Cucurbita pepo). Family: Cucurbit.
Companions: Corn, Beans, Radishes, Marigold, Nasturtium, Peas.
Antagonists: Potatoes, Broccoli, Cabbage.
Why companions help: Large leaves shade soil and suppress weeds; Three Sisters planting provides mutual benefits; Nasturtiums trap aphids
Why avoid antagonists: Potatoes compete for nutrients and share diseases; Brassicas compete for soil nutrients
Growing: Full sun, water High, spacing 36 inches, Heavy feeder.
Attracts: Bees.

Plant: Pepper (Capsicum annuum). Family: Nightshade.
Companions: Basil, Tomatoes, Carrots, Onions, Parsley, Spinach, Marigold.
Antagonists: Fennel, Kohlrabi, Beans, Brassicas.
Why companions help: Basil repels aphids and spider mites; Carrots loosen soil; Onions deter pests; Marigolds repel nematodes
Why avoid antagonists: Fennel inhibits growth; Beans compete for nutrients; Kohlrabi stunts pepper growth
Growing: Full sun, water Medium, spacing 18 inches, Heavy feeder.
Attracts: Bees.

Plant: Onions (Allium cepa). Family: Allium.
Companions: Carrots, Lettuce, Tomatoes, Beets, Cabbage, Strawberries, Peppers.
Antagonists: Beans, Peas, Asparagus, Sage.
Why companions help: Strong scent deters many pests; Carrots and onions mask each other's scent from pests; Repels carrot fly and aphids
Why avoid antagonists: Inhibits growth of beans and peas; Competes with asparagus
Growing: Full sun, water Low, spacing 4 inches, Light feeder.
Pests deterred: Carrot fly, Aphids, Japanese beetles, Rabbits.

Plant: Garlic (Allium sativum). Family: Allium.
Companions: Tomatoes, Peppers, Cabbage, Carrots, Fruit trees, Roses.
Antagonists: Beans, Peas, Asparagus, Strawberries.
Why companions help: Deters aphids, Japanese beetles, spider mites; Strong scent masks other plants from pests; Natural fungicide properties
Why avoid antagonists: Inhibits growth of legumes; May affect strawberry growth
Growing: Full sun, water Low, spacing 6 inches, Light feeder.
Pests deterred: Aphids, Japanese beetles, Spider mites, Fungal diseases.

Plant: Lettuce (Lactuca sativa). Family: Aster.
Companions: Carrots, Radishes, Strawberries, Chives, Onions, Garlic, Beans.
Antagonists: Celery.
Why companions help: Shallow roots don't compete with deeper-rooted plants; Provides ground cover; Grows well in shade of taller plants
Why avoid antagonists: Celery may inhibit lettuce growth
Growing: Partial sun, water Medium, spacing 6 inches, Light feeder.

Plant: Cucumber (Cucumis sativus). Family: Cucurbit.
Companions: Beans, Corn, Peas, Radishes, Sunflowers, Lettuce, Dill.
Antagonists: Potatoes, Sage, Mint, Melons.
Why companions help: Beans and peas provide nitrogen; Corn provides trellis; Radishes deter cucumber beetles; Sunflowers attract pollinators
Why avoid antagonists: Potatoes compete and share diseases; Aromatic herbs may inhibit growth; Melons cross-pollinate
Growing: Full sun, water High, spacing 12 inches, Heavy feeder.
Attracts: Bees.

Plant: Peas (Pisum sativum). Family: Legume.
Companions: Carrots, Turnips, Radishes, Cucumber, Corn, Beans, Spinach.
Antagonists: Onions, Garlic, Chives, Leeks.
Why companions help: Fixes nitrogen in soil; Provides shade for cool-season crops; Good trellis partner with corn
Why avoid antagonists: Alliums inhibit pea growth
Growing: Full sun, water Medium, spacing 3 inches, Nitrogen fixer.
Attracts: Bees.

Plant: Broccoli (Brassica oleracea var. italica). Family: Brassica.
Companions: Beets, Celery, Onions, Oregano, Potatoes, Chamomile, Dill, Rosemary.
Antagonists: Tomatoes, Peppers, Strawberries, Beans, Squash.
Why companions help: Celery repels cabbage white butterfly; Aromatic herbs deter pests; Onions mask brassica scent
Why avoid antagonists: Tomatoes and peppers compete for calcium; Strawberries inhibit brassica growth; Heavy feeders compete
Growing: Full sun, water High, spacing 18 inches, Heavy feeder.

Plant: Cabbage (Brassica oleracea var. capitata). Family: Brassica.
Companions: Onions, Celery, Dill, Chamomile, Beets, Potatoes, Garlic.
Antagonists: Tomatoes, Peppers, Strawberries, Beans, Grapes.
Why companions help: Aromatic herbs deter cabbage moths and loopers; Onions repel many pests; Chamomile improves flavor
Why avoid antagonists: Tomatoes and cabbage compete for nutrients; Strawberries inhibit growth; Beans compete
Growing: Full sun, water High, spacing 18 inches, Heavy feeder.

Plant: Spinach (Spinacia oleracea). Family: Amaranth.
Companions: Strawberries, Beans, Peas, Cabbage, Radishes, Onions, Cauliflower.
Antagonists: Potatoes.
Why companions help: Provides ground cover and shades soil; Grows well in shade of taller plants; Legumes provide nitrogen
Why avoid antagonists: Potatoes may inhibit spinach growth
Growing: Partial sun, water Medium, spacing 6 inches, Light feeder.

Plant: Radishes (Raphanus sativus). Family: Brassica.
Companions: Lettuce, Peas, Nasturtium, Cucumber, Squash, Beans, Carrots.
Antagonists: Hyssop, Grapes.
Why companions help: Fast-growing trap crop for flea beetles; Loosens soil for root vegetables; Marks slow-germinating seeds
Why avoid antagonists: Hyssop inhibits radish growth
Growing: Full sun, water Medium, spacing 2 inches, Light feeder.
Pests deterred: Cucumber beetles.

Plant: Zucchini (Cucurbita pepo var. cylindrica). Family: Cucurbit.
Companions: Corn, Beans, Nasturtium, Radishes, Marigold, Peas.
Antagonists: Potatoes, Pumpkins, Other squash varieties.
Why companions help: Large leaves shade soil and suppress weeds; Corn provides windbreak; Beans fix nitrogen
Why avoid antagonists: Potatoes share diseases; Cross-pollinates with other squash varieties
Growing: Full sun, water High, spacing 36 inches, Heavy feeder.
Attracts: Bees.

Plant: Marigold (Tagetes erecta). Family: Aster.
Companions: Tomatoes, Peppers, Cucumbers, Squash, Melons, Potatoes, Zucchini.
Antagonists: Beans, Cabbage.
Why companions help: Repels nematodes, aphids, whiteflies, and many pests; Attracts beneficial insects; Root secretions deter harmful soil organisms
Why avoid antagonists: May inhibit bean growth; Some brassicas don't benefit
Growing: Full sun, water Low, spacing 8 inches, Light feeder.
Pests deterred: Nematodes, Aphids, Whiteflies, Tomato hornworm, Cabbage moths.
Attracts: Bees, Butterflies, Ladybugs, Hoverflies.
